footnotes.text: Bishop: represents a Greek word meaning "overseer," and "presbyter" another Greek word meaning, "elder." In St. Paul "bishop" and "presbyter" seem to be used convertibly, and probably priests are here included under the term "bishops."
Married but once: priestly celibacy as a law is of later ecclesiastical institution. No condemnation of second marriages in general is intended.
Deacons: though this term is used in other places in the general sense of minister or servant, here it clearly refers to the sacramental office and order.
